guardrail: static projection of the public register; no facts added here

data.name: European Union -> United States (EUR->USD settlement)
data.feasibility_class: Regime-in-transition (destination regime enacted, not yet operative; resolves to Category II on the earlier of 2027-01-18 or 120 days after final rules)

divergence.reason: Directed-edge operative interaction sets (Corridor Atlas v0.2.5 §5, read at the destination inbound gate for THIS direction) differ from the undirected pairwise sets (Architecture §5.14). The Atlas generalizes the §5.12/§5.14 undirected pairs into directed edges and re-derives the operative interaction sets per direction; the compatibility CATEGORY agrees, the interaction-set list may differ by direction. This divergence is declared, not silent.

infrastructure_overlap.note: Both ends on Project Agora (EU and US).

inbound_mechanism.test: GENIUS Act §18 comparability determination + OCC registration; US-held reserves — enacted 2025-07-18, gate not yet operative (§20)
inbound_mechanism.detail: The §18 pathway (unilateral Treasury comparability determination on SCRC recommendation + OCC registration + US-held reserves) is NOT YET AVAILABLE: per §20 the Act takes effect on the earlier of 2027-01-18 (18 months after enactment) or 120 days after the primary federal payment stablecoin regulators issue final regulations, and at the 2026-06-30 snapshot only proposed rules had issued (OCC NPRM, FR 2026-03-02, comments closed 2026-05-01; FDIC and FinCEN/OFAC NPRMs 2026-04-10; Treasury §4(c) NPRM April 2026). Earlier prose read ‘none granted to date’, which states a MARKET observation (no determination has been made) where a proposition of law is required (the gate is not in force) — the claim-class error Citable by Construction §2.1 exists to prevent.
inbound_mechanism.administrator: US Treasury (Stablecoin Certification Review Committee) / OCC

data.note: The asymmetric mirror of the dollar-into-Europe corridor, gated differently. A MiCA-authorized euro issuer does not thereby gain US access; entry turns on a §18 Treasury comparability determination, OCC registration, and US-held reserves. The §18 gate is not yet in force (§20: effective on the earlier of 2027-01-18 or final rules + 120 days), so the edge is regime-in-transition today and resolves to Category II on commencement. ‘No determination has been granted’ is a market observation; ‘the gate is not yet available’ is the proposition of law. The asymmetry is structural — the comparability gate is unilateral with no MiCA counterpart.

source.primary: Fan, Cross-Border Digital-Finance Corridor Atlas v0.2.5 (June 2026), §5.2 (European Union -> United States)
source.secondary: Fan, Cross-Border Stablecoin Architecture v3 (Twelve Jurisdictions, June 2026), §5.14 (EU x US: Category I/II hybrid)

class_basis.instrument: GENIUS Act s.18 (foreign payment-stablecoin issuer comparability determination by the Secretary of the Treasury on SCRC recommendation + OCC registration + US-held reserves), effective per s.20 on the earlier of 18 months after enactment (2027-01-18) or 120 days after the primary federal payment stablecoin regulators issue final regulations.

record_ref_meta.note: `us-pss-monetary_sovereignty-001` cites the GENIUS Act, pinpoint 'Foreign payment stablecoin issuers — comparability determination and registration' — the same §18 gate the signal names. The record codes the ACT as in_force_enacted (it is: enacted 2025-07-18); the signal codes the GATE, which §20 makes effective on the earlier of 2027-01-18 or final-rules + 120 days. Act-enacted is not section-operative. This is errata E1 / VB-06, and it is the divergence that moves the 8 inbound-US edges from II to T.
record_ref_meta.divergence: `us-pss-monetary_sovereignty-001` cites the GENIUS Act, pinpoint 'Foreign payment stablecoin issuers — comparability determination and registration' — the same §18 gate the signal names. The record codes the ACT as in_force_enacted (it is: enacted 2025-07-18); the signal codes the GATE, which §20 makes effective on the earlier of 2027-01-18 or final-rules + 120 days. Act-enacted is not section-operative. This is errata E1 / VB-06, and it is the divergence that moves the 8 inbound-US edges from II to T.

scheduled.basis: GENIUS Act s.18 (foreign payment-stablecoin issuer comparability determination by the Secretary of the Treasury on SCRC recommendation + OCC registration + US-held reserves), effective per s.20 on the earlier of 18 months after enactment (2027-01-18) or 120 days after the primary federal payment stablecoin regulators issue final regulations.
